element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core interaction throughout - raising and educating Pino via Tinker interface
Primary interaction mechanic - player uses Tinker as cursor to interface with world
Building foundation for challenge success - trainable via items and tasks
Quick parameter adjustments for health/trust management
Teaching Pino specific abilities for upcoming challenges
Managed through physical tasks, climbing, injuries - restored via sleep
Managed through mental tasks, reading, social interactions - restored via sleep
Parameter-gated tasks that unlock character development progression
Culminating objective requiring well-developed parameters - enables final task and human-Gijin relations outcome
Time-gated tasks that gate certain content and require planning
Health restoration with tradeoff (money cost, day advancement) - core resource management

typical_relief_triggers: Sleep/rest to restore both health bars
Successful challenge completion unlocking virtue circuits
Consumable item use for quick parameter recovery
Passage of time enabling time-gated tasks
typical_tension_triggers: Parameter-gated challenges the player is underprepared for
Accumulating mental fatigue from reading/cognitive tasks
Physical damage from climbing or confrontations
Dual health bar degradation from combined tasks
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical with daily rhythm - tension builds through task attempts and health degradation; relief comes through sleep and successful challenge completion. Pacing governed by character development gates (virtue circuits) and time-based mechanics (farming, sailing). Replay incentive adds secondary rhythm: speedrun completion within day limits for secret ending.

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Secret ending unlocked by completing each act within specific day limits - hidden progression path encourages replay and time-optimized play

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to dual health system and parameter-based challenge gating. Player learns scolding/praising mechanic and begins collecting training items. Low barrier to entry with safe introduction contexts.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Accumulation of virtue circuits requiring increasingly developed parameters. Time-gated content (farming, sailing) introduces scheduling complexity. Mental and physical health management becomes more demanding as tasks compound both bars.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Circuit J quest line requires well-balanced character parameters across multiple dimensions. Final task determines human-Gijin relations outcome. Parameter thresholds become harder to meet without strategic item collection and task sequencing.
notable_spikes: First virtue circuit requiring parameters player may not have prioritized
Time-gated tasks (farming/sailing) if player lacks sufficient strength or specific trained behaviors
Circuit J final quest - comprehensive parameter check across all dimensions
Secret ending requirement - must complete each act within strict day limits, creating speedrun difficulty spike

notable_level_patterns.description: Each in-game day represents a tension-relief cycle. Player attempts challenges, accumulates health damage, then sleeps to reset. Day counter only affects time-gated content, allowing flexible pacing while maintaining progress structure.

Virtue circuits act as skill-check gates. Player must achieve specific parameter thresholds (strength, kindness, luck, etc.) to unlock challenges. Failure encourages item collection and targeted parameter building.

Physical and mental health as separate but interconnected resources. Some tasks degrade only one; others degrade both. Sleep is the only restoration method. Creates resource scarcity and forces planning.
Teaching Pino specific skills (sword fighting, seed sowing) via item possession before he can use them in challenges. Creates mini-progression loops within larger narrative arc.
Post-game incentive: complete each act within time limits for secret ending. Transforms second playthrough into time-optimized challenge run, adding meta-rhythm of speedrunning optimization.

sources_used: Wikipedia - Wonder Project J article (Gameplay, Synopsis, Development and release sections)
